questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: Which of the following best describes India's specific historical contributions to the evolution of UN Peacekeeping operations as mentioned in recent reports?

options.text: India is the only country to have provided a permanent training facility for the Department of Peace Operations (DPO).
India was the first nation to deploy an all-women Formed Police Unit to a UN peacekeeping mission.
India holds the distinction of being the first country to lead the United Nations Truce Supervision Organization (UNTSO).
India is the primary architect of the Capstone Doctrine adopted by the UN in 2005.

questions.explanation: India made history in 2007 by becoming the first country to deploy an all-women Formed Police Unit (FPU) to the UN mission in Liberia. While India has the CUNPK for training, it is a national focal point, not the 'only' facility for the DPO. UNTSO was established in 1948, and the Capstone Doctrine is a UN-wide doctrine, not attributed solely to India.
questions.takeaway: India's contribution to UN Peacekeeping is notable for pioneering gender parity through the first all-women Formed Police Unit.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: India became the first country to deploy an all-women Formed Police Unit (2007, Liberia). India's Centre for United Nations Peacekeeping (CUNPK), established by the Indian Army in New Delhi, serves as the nation's focal point for peacekeeping training.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: In the context of international law and fugitive offenders, India has recently invoked which of the following UN frameworks for extradition requests?

options.text: The Capstone Doctrine and the Responsibility to Protect (R2P)
The UN Convention against Transnational Organized Crime (UNTOC) and the UN Convention against Corruption (UNCAC)
The UN Charter and the Universal Declaration of Human Rights (UDHR)
The Rome Statute and the UN Security Council Resolution 1267

questions.explanation: According to the source material, India has recently invoked the United Nations Convention against Transnational Organized Crime (UNTOC) and the United Nations Convention against Corruption (UNCAC) in its extradition requests for fugitive offenders.
questions.takeaway: UNTOC and UNCAC serve as critical legal instruments for international cooperation in extraditing individuals involved in organized crime and corruption.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Recently, UNTOC and UNCAC have been invoked by India in the extradition request of a fugitive offender. The United Nations Convention against Transnational Organized Crime (UNTOC) ... entered into force in 2003.
